topic: EXP-289 data-source parenthetical updated to Schumann-domain sources
change_summary: EXP-289 substring replace in detail_verdict_text: '(IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.)' → 'Schumann resonance observatory records — e.g. Nagycenk HU, NCAR/Sondrestrom, Maitri-IIG networks'. Wrapper sentence unchanged. ISS-1863/ISS-1864 cleanup.
our_argument_summary: WIN-002 verdict 'Self-Contradicted' rests on three internal-consistency failures: dome cavity geometry H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) gives ~22 Hz, not 7.83 Hz; the 10.59 Hz 'raw theoretical' is Schumann's 1952 globe formula not a dome derivation; WIN-029's Schumann-derived height (9,572 km) exceeds the dome's own ceiling (8,537 km) by 12%. Detail_verdict_text adds the post-hoc-test wrapper. EXP-289 patches the data-source parenthetical from generic to Schumann-specific.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: EXP-289 is a string_replace_first_occurrence operation on detail_verdict_text. It does not touch detail_evidence, detail_claim, finding, claim, verdict, or any code_analysis tag. The wrapper sentence ('The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset ... that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit ...') is preserved verbatim. Patch arithmetic verified by reading WIN-002.detail_verdict_text in the fresh clone — the new parenthetical is present and the wrapper is intact.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: n/a — patch is a static-substring substitution.
data_sources_used: Nagycenk Geodetic and Geophysical Observatory (Hungary) — long-running Schumann ELF station
NCAR / Sondrestrom (Greenland) — primarily incoherent-scatter radar; Schumann coverage is secondary at this site (see hole #1)
Maitri-IIG (Indian Institute of Geomagnetism, Antarctic station) — operates Schumann ELF instruments

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Per prior cycles, monitor.py hardcodes 7.83 Hz target; tag preserved (untouched by EXP-289).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Schumann's 1952 globe-cavity formula already explains the 7.83 Hz fundamental; dome relabels as 'aetheric damping'. Tag preserved (untouched by EXP-289).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Schumann fundamental measured since 1952; dome adopted the value. Tag preserved.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Dome cavity geometry gives ~22 Hz, not 7.83 Hz — value adopted from globe formula, not derived from dome geometry. Tag preserved.

holes_found.description: Sondrestrom is primarily known as the NCAR Upper Atmospheric Research Facility (incoherent-scatter radar / ionospheric studies), not as a flagship Schumann ELF observatory. Listing it alongside Nagycenk and Maitri-IIG as 'Schumann resonance observatory records' overstates Sondrestrom's identity in this domain. A more conventional ELF/SLF observatory list would feature Nagycenk (HU), Lehta (RU), the Hollister/Parkfield (US) ELF arrays, Tomsk (RU) — the Tomsk Space Observation System is even named in raw-text/02-wins.txt for PROS-008 Schumann persistence — or Maitri-IIG. Substituting one of those for Sondrestrom would be more rigorous. Not blocking — Sondrestrom does have ELF capability, just not as its primary identity.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider replacing 'NCAR/Sondrestrom' with 'Tomsk Space Observation System' or 'Lehta (Karelia)' to lean on stations whose Schumann identity is unambiguous. The dome's own raw-text already cites Tomsk for the same frequency band.

holes_found.description: The 'e.g.' framing makes the list non-exhaustive, which is good — a defender cannot demand a specific entry. The earlier IGRF-style generic list invited attack ('IGRF doesn't measure Schumann') and the new domain-specific list defuses that. The remaining critique surface is now narrowed to whether each entry is in fact a Schumann observatory (see hole #1).

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly cites the Schumann fundamental as ~7.83 Hz, which IS a real, robust, decades-old measurement (data/wins.json WIN-002 detail_evidence references Schumann's 1952 cavity formula). Schumann observatory networks (Nagycenk, Maitri-IIG, Tomsk, Lehta) measure this frequency continuously and the 7.83 Hz value is one of the more solid empirical anchors in atmospheric physics. The data is genuinely 'public and field-specific' — exactly the post-hoc-test wrapper's claim.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: But the WIN's claim is not about the 7.83 Hz value per se — it's about a 26% aetheric damping coefficient derived from a 10.59 Hz / 7.83 Hz gap, where 10.59 Hz is Schumann's own globe-cavity prediction (data/wins.json WIN-002 finding) and the dome cavity geometry independently gives ~22 Hz (data/wins.json WIN-002 finding). The patched parenthetical doesn't address that contradiction — it only sharpens the post-hoc-test paragraph by replacing a generic source list with a domain-appropriate one. Self-contradiction stands.

deeper_analysis:
BATCHED REVIEW (1 of 2). Companion: WIN-060.

Scope of the patch: substring replacement in detail_verdict_text. The wrapper sentence — which is the actual load-bearing part of the post-hoc-test argument — is unchanged. Detail_evidence, detail_claim, finding, claim, verdict, code_analysis tags: all unchanged.

PATCH CORRECTNESS: confirmed by reading WIN-002 detail_verdict_text in the fresh clone — the new Schumann-domain parenthetical is present, the surrounding wrapper ('The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset ... that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit ...') is intact, and no other field was disturbed.

PER-WIN DOMAIN CHECK (the per-WIN attention the decider explicitly requested):
- Nagycenk HU: real, well-known Schumann ELF station — appropriate ✓
- NCAR/Sondrestrom: Sondrestrom is primarily an upper-atmospheric/ionospheric incoherent-scatter radar facility (NCAR-affiliated). Schumann ELF coverage exists but isn't its primary identity. Listing it as a top-three Schumann observatory overstates its role in this domain. Imprecise but not factually wrong — flagged minor.
- Maitri-IIG: IIG operates Schumann instruments at the Indian Antarctic station Maitri — appropriate ✓

ADVOCATE-MODE: a dome defender's best counter is that the OLD parenthetical was harder to attack because it didn't commit to specifics ('IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.'). True in a perverse sense, but that defense relies on the old list being so off-domain that it's defensible-by-vagueness — not a sustainable position. The new list, with the Sondrestrom imprecision, is still net-improved: at minimum two of three entries are unambiguous Schumann observatories.

VERDICT MISMATCH: Verdict 'Self-Contradicted' continues to rest on the 22 Hz vs 10.59 Hz vs 7.83 Hz triple inconsistency (data/wins.json WIN-002 finding) and WIN-029's 9,572 km vs dome's 8,537 km ceiling. The detail_verdict_text patch is in the post-hoc-test paragraph, not the self-contradiction analysis. Verdict unaffected.

NO BATCH-CONTAMINATION CHECK: this WIN's tags and verdict are unrelated to WIN-060's. The two WINs sit on different physical phenomena (Schumann ELF cavity vs SAA westward magnetic drift). The shared property is only the EXP-289 templated wrapper sentence.

citations_verified: data/wins.json WIN-002 detail_verdict_text — new Schumann-source parenthetical present
data/wins.json WIN-002 detail_verdict_text — wrapper sentence intact
citations_unchecked: Nagycenk Schumann ELF station catalog — site is real and well-known but not directly re-verified this cycle
Sondrestrom Schumann coverage — primary identity is upper-atmospheric radar, not ELF; the 'NCAR/Sondrestrom' framing is potentially imprecise
Maitri-IIG Schumann instruments — IIG involvement at Maitri is well documented but not directly re-verified

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender's strongest line on the patch itself: 'You\'re changing the parenthetical to a more domain-specific list, but Sondrestrom isn\'t primarily a Schumann observatory — you traded a generic but defensible list for a specific but partly-imprecise one. The previous IGRF/etc. list was harder to attack because it didn\'t commit to specifics.'
advocate_mode.rebuttal: The previous IGRF/etc. parenthetical was attackable on the OPPOSITE ground (it listed sources that have nothing to do with Schumann ELF). The new list is more defensible-on-direction even with the Sondrestrom imprecision: at minimum two of three (Nagycenk, Maitri-IIG) are unambiguously Schumann observatories, and adding Tomsk (already cited in raw-text for PROS-008) would close the imprecision. Net: the patch makes the post-hoc-test argument more credible, not less. The hole is cosmetic, not structural.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: None introduced by the patch. EXP-289 only touches detail_verdict_text parentheticals; cross-WIN tag relations and verdict relations are unaffected.

summary_for_decider: EXP-289 substring replace on WIN-002 detail_verdict_text is bit-correct and preserves the wrapper sentence. New parenthetical is largely domain-appropriate (Nagycenk and Maitri-IIG are bona fide Schumann ELF stations); 'NCAR/Sondrestrom' is the weakest entry — Sondrestrom's primary identity is incoherent-scatter radar, not Schumann. Recommend a future minor edit to replace Sondrestrom with Tomsk (already cited in raw-text/02-wins.txt for PROS-008) or Lehta. recommended_action: no_change for this batch — verdict 'Self-Contradicted' unaffected; the imprecision is cosmetic.
